get.summary: Get the thumbnail info for a document in the default workspace.
get.description:
* By default, returns thumbnail info for the element with the most-recently generated image. If you pinned an element for the document thumbnail, that element will always be used for the document-level thumbnail, if it exists in the workspace.
* The default workspace may vary by user; the image served depends on the signed-in user.
* See also: [Tech tip on how to change a document thumbnail in onshape](https://www.onshape.com/en/resource-center/tech-tips/tech-tip-how-to-change-a-document-thumbnail-in-onshape)

get.summary: Get the thumbnail info for a version of a document.
get.description:
* By default, returns thumbnail info for the element with the most-recently generated image. If you pinned an element for the document thumbnail, that element will always be used for the document-level thumbnail, if it exists in the workspace.
* See also: [Tech tip on how to change a document thumbnail in onshape](https://www.onshape.com/en/resource-center/tech-tips/tech-tip-how-to-change-a-document-thumbnail-in-onshape)

get.summary: Get the thumbnail info for a workspace.
get.description:
* By default, returns thumbnail info for the element with the most-recently generated image. If you pinned an element for the document thumbnail, that element will always be used for the document-level thumbnail, if it exists in the workspace.
* See also: [Tech tip on how to change a document thumbnail in onshape](https://www.onshape.com/en/resource-center/tech-tips/tech-tip-how-to-change-a-document-thumbnail-in-onshape)

get.summary: Get the thumbnail image with the given configuration for an element.
get.description: Returns the thumbnail image for an element at a specified version, with the given configuration.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The id of the document through which the above document should be accessed; only applicable when accessing a version of the document. This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.name: wid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: eid
parameters.description: The id of the element in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: sz
parameters.description: the generated thumbnail size in pixels, widthxheigth

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: Cache Control key. If specified, the response header returned will tell the client to use cached thumbnails.

parameters.name: skipDefaultImage
parameters.description: Controls the return of the default image, if thumbnail is not available

By default return the default thumbnail,.summary: Get the default image, if the thumbnail is not found
By default return the default thumbnail,.description: By default return the default thumbnail, if the thumbnail is not found or generated

Setting skipDefaultImage=true, returns a.summary: Skip the default image, if the thumbnail does not exist.
Setting skipDefaultImage=true, returns a.description: Setting skipDefaultImage=true, returns a 404 if the thumbnail is not found

get.summary: Get the thumbnail image with the given size for a document.
get.description:
* By default, returns thumbnail image for the element with the most-recently generated image. If you pinned an element for the document thumbnail, that element will always be used for the document-level thumbnail, if it exists in the workspace.
* See also: [Tech tip on how to change a document thumbnail in onshape](https://www.onshape.com/en/resource-center/tech-tips/tech-tip-how-to-change-a-document-thumbnail-in-onshape)

parameters.name: sz
parameters.description: the generated thumbnail size in pixels, widthxheigth

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: Cache Control key. If specified, the response header returned will tell the client to use cached thumbnails.

parameters.name: skipDefaultImage
parameters.description: Controls the return of the default image, if thumbnail is not available

By default return the default thumbnail,.summary: Get the default image, if the thumbnail is not found
By default return the default thumbnail,.description: By default return the default thumbnail, if the thumbnail is not found or generated

Setting skipDefaultImage=true, returns a.summary: Skip the default image, if the thumbnail does not exist.
Setting skipDefaultImage=true, returns a.description: Setting skipDefaultImage=true, returns a 404 if the thumbnail is not found

get.summary: Get the thumbnail info structure for an element.
get.description: Returns thumbnail info for the given document, workspace or version, and element.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The id of the document through which the above document should be accessed; only applicable when accessing a version of the document. This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.name: wv
parameters.description: Indicates which of workspace (w) or version (v) id is specified below.

parameters.name: wvid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace, version in which the operation should be performed.

parameters.name: eid
parameters.description: The id of the element in which to perform the operation.

post.summary: Set the thumbnail image for an application element.
post.description:
* Allows 3rd-party applications to set thumbnails for their elements. 
* Application elements can have both primary and secondary thumbnails. A primary thumbnail represents the top-level of the element. A secondary thumbnail can represent sub-components of the element (e.g., a drawing sheet).
* To update one or more thumbnails, you must set the overwrite query param to `true` and supply the entire set of thumbnails. All previous thumbnails will be deleted prior to updating the element with the latest images.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The id of the document through which the above document should be accessed; only applicable when accessing a version of the document. This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.name: wv
parameters.description: Indicates which of workspace (w) or version (v) id is specified below.

parameters.name: wvid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace, version in which the operation should be performed.

parameters.name: eid
parameters.description: The id of the element in which to perform the operation.

delete.description: Deletes an application element's thumbnail and images for the given document, workspace or version, and element combination.

get.summary: This endpoint will be deprecated soon. Use `getThumbnailForDocument` instead.
get.description: This API exists for historical reasons. It uses `/document/` in the path, rather than the standard `/d/` to specify the document.

get.summary: This endpoint will be deprecated soon. Use `getThumbnailForDocumentAndVersion` instead.
get.description: This API exists for historical reasons. It uses `/document/` in the path, rather than the standard `/d/` to specify the document.

scopes.OAuth2ReadPII: Application can read your profile information
scopes.OAuth2Write: Application can write to your documents
scopes.OAuth2Delete: Application can delete your documents and workspaces
scopes.OAuth2Purchase: Application can request purchases on your behalf
scopes.OAuth2Share: Application can share and unshare documents on your behalf
scopes.document.create: Atlas Application can create a document
scopes.document.edit: Atlas Application can edit a document
scopes.document.delete: Atlas Application can delete a document
scopes.document.read: Atlas Application can read a document
scopes.enterprise.create: Atlas Application can create an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.read: Atlas Application can read from an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.edit: Atlas Application can edit an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.delete: Atlas Application can delete an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.member.create: Atlas Application can create an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.read: Atlas Application can read the details of an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.edit: Atlas Application can edit the details of an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.delete: Atlas Application can delete a member from an enterprise
scopes.webhook.create: Atlas Application can create a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.edit: Atlas Application can edit a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.read: Atlas Application can read a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.delete: Atlas Application can delete a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.PLMIntegration: PLM automation can invoke limited operations
